transcript.text: Hello, I'm Kyle Pounds, and this video is what I think about immigration.

And it's kind of like, well, are we American or are we becoming like Mexican?
So, well, number one, first thing that's coming through my mind is I don't think that all this Mexican immigration was an accident by those in powers that be.
I think they wanted those Mexicans to come in here.
I think we usually could have built a fence a long time ago.
I don't think it's that expensive.
It's just a couple thousand miles with all the money we spend on all sorts of other stuff.
We usually could have built a fence by now, and then it would have stopped those Mexicans from coming in.
How do I feel about all the Mexicans coming in?
Well, I think Mexican, let's not forget that this whole continent of America was the redskin.
Mexicans represent redskins probably as much as any, almost as much or as much as any other country,
Along with like Guatemala, Peru, Bolivia, and Mexico.
So those are really the people who have lived here since the beginning of time, you know, for a long time.
There's only a million, at least as of 10 years ago, a million Native Americans left in the United States of America.
So I see it kind of as like a reinvigorating of the red race and their home continent, which I like.
I like Mexicans.
I like the Indians.
They're very soft, mellow, kind, humble in a way that could benefit us.
And they come over here.
I think their effect over here works great for us white and, you know, black and us Native Americans here

And they're not going to come up and challenge us for jobs or power anytime soon.
I think it seems to me, I mean, I don't have that much interaction with them,
But it seems to me like a lot of Mexicans aren't learning English.
And that'll put a hamper in their abilities to grow up.
Maybe, yeah, I mean, they will eventually, but I don't think it's their fault.
A lot of them aren't learning English, though.
I think a lot of them are peasants, which, you know, the dictionary definition is illiterate farmer.
They're just illiterate Mexicans.
They don't even read Spanish in their own country.

So it kind of makes it impossible for them to learn English once they get over here.
But they will.
So what should we do now, though?
What should our policy be about immigration?
I think we should stop Mexican immigration.
I think we've had enough of it.
But also, I think we should stop Mexican immigration because it's illegal.
And it kind of undermines our whole legal system.
We should be able to treat them like regular people if they're going to be here.
Tax them, make money off of taxing them.
If they're working under the table, they don't have as many rights as they should.
You know, humans, too.
They're not like slave class.
We're not trying to create a slave class.
And if we need workers over here, well, we let them apply and we take the best people who are qualified.
You know, we can pay for that bureaucracy.
That's not too difficult.
So make it legal, but don't let them illegally come in.
You're just pissing the face of the law.
And we could easily stop them, I think.
Just build a fence.
I guess, and I heard, I think, like 66% of the people in like 50 years or something are going to be Latinos.
But I don't think Spanish will ever be a stronger language in English.
English is already the world language.
Spanish, and I think that would be a good thing if it was kind of a bilingual country where a lot of people speak Spanish.
I think it's good for your brain and your mind and everything to be able to speak two languages.
But, yeah, it will never challenge English, so I don't see that as a problem.
However, I do not think that public schools should teach in Spanish, teach regular classes in Spanish just because there's Mexican-American kids over here who haven't learned English.
That's just unacceptable.
The first thing you need to learn is English if you're going to try and get an education.
Don't try and learn about fossils and the history of the world and your own language.
And use our taxpayers' money doing it when you're probably not even paying the taxes because you're working illegally.
I really think people need to learn English, like, focus all their energy on learning English, and then they study school and English.

It's like you were saying.
